a: Learn how Rosetta translates executables, and understand what Rosetta can’t translate.

t: Adapting iOS code to run in the macOS environment
a: Support modern iOS features that result in a better user experience when running on Apple silicon.

t: Addressing architectural differences in your macOS code
a: Fix problems that stem from architectural differences between Apple silicon and Intel-based Mac computers.

t: Apple Silicon CPU Optimization Guide Version 4
a: Identify performance optimization strategies for Apple silicon M-series and A-series chips.
Get the resources you need to create software for Macs with Apple silicon.
Create macOS apps and other executables that run natively on both Apple silicon and Intel-based Mac computers.
Configure your system to enable the debugging of custom kernel extensions from a second Mac.

t: Improving control flow integrity with pointer authentication
a: Increase confidence that your code uses pointers correctly.
Install kernel extensions using a custom installer package, and help users understand the installation process.

t: Porting just-in-time compilers to Apple silicon
a: Update your just-in-time (JIT) compiler to work with the Hardened Runtime capability, and with Apple silicon.

t: Porting your Metal code to Apple silicon
a: Create a version of your Metal app that runs on both Apple silicon and Intel-based Mac computers.

t: Porting your audio code to Apple silicon
a: Eliminate issues in your audio-specific code when running on Apple silicon Mac computers.

t: Porting your macOS apps to Apple silicon
a: Create a version of your macOS app that runs on both Apple silicon and Intel-based Mac computers.

t: Providing an edge-to-edge, full-screen experience in your iPad app running on a Mac
a: Take advantage of the true native resolution of a Mac display when running your iPad app in full-screen mode on a Mac.

t: Providing touch gesture equivalents using Touch Alternatives
a: Enable Touch Alternatives to provide keyboard, mouse, and trackpad equivalents to your iOS app when it runs on a Mac with Apple silicon.

t: Running your iOS apps in macOS
a: Modernize the iOS apps you choose to run on a Mac with Apple silicon, or opt out of running on a Mac altogether.

t: Tuning your code’s performance for Apple silicon
a: Improve your code to get the best performance from both Apple silicon and Intel-based Mac computers.
